user_outcome: A SwornAgent user upgrades from the shared credential envelope without losing provider keys; an unused issuer-less legacy session is safely removed, and only a fresh issuer-bound login can create account state.
rationale: The current field-preserving envelope contains unrelated secrets under one unversioned corruption and logout boundary. This slice owns C-01 and the storage boundary for C-02, preserves the provider file path for compatibility, and makes the split converge through a verified provider rewrite without guessing authority for an issuer-less bearer token.
in_scope: Define and validate provider-credentials-v1 at the existing credentials.json path and account-session-v1 at account.json, each written 0600 inside a 0700 configuration directory.
Replace field-name mutation as the long-term authority with record-specific stores that reject malformed or unsupported versions without overwriting source bytes.

Implement one idempotent migration routine that validates the legacy composite, requires recognized non-account fields to be consumed by their owning migrations and rejects unknown fields, constructs a versioned provider record preserving the complete providers map, writes it to a 0600 temporary file, syncs it, re-reads and deep-compares the map, then atomically replaces credentials.json.
Discard issuer-less legacy token, email, tier, and expires_at fields only at the verified atomic replacement; create no account.json and report that a fresh sworn login is required.
Use the original composite as the recovery source before replacement; converge every declared interruption point and leave no temporary or permanent token-bearing recovery material after replacement.

Preserve providers, permissions, and canonical provider environment precedence throughout migration and later record-specific writes; require S05 to consume legacy webhook_url before the provider rewrite and fail closed on any unclaimed field.

Make sworn doctor report absent, legacy, migrated, conflicting, corrupt, and unsupported layouts without printing field values; make its fix path call the same migration routine used by account-aware commands.
Keep provider-only readers and commands independent of account migration and account-record validity.
out_of_scope: Device login, issuer validation, account output, and logout gestures belong to S02-authoritative-account-session.
Model routing and proxy removal belong to S03-direct-routing-only.
Legacy webhook_url migration and notifications.json belong to S05-independent-webhook-consent.
OS keyrings, hardware-backed secret storage, and a general encrypted state database are not introduced.

effort_complexity.rationale: Two live secret formats, crash ordering, compatibility, cache freshness, permissions, and recovery behavior create a wide and failure-sensitive migration surface.

risks.description: A crash during provider-record replacement loses a provider key or strands an ambiguous temporary record.
risks.mitigation: Use named kill points before temporary write, after write, after sync, after re-read verification, and after atomic rename; every fixture must converge on retry with the provider map unchanged and no unclaimed field discarded.

risks.description: A migration backup or quarantine becomes a permanent second copy of the issuer-less account token.
risks.mitigation: Create no backup or quarantine: the source composite remains authoritative before rename, the verified C-01 record is authoritative after rename, and no temporary file may remain after completion.

risks.description: A new binary writes a provider record an older binary can no longer read.
risks.mitigation: Keep credentials.json and the providers field stable; version is additive and an old-reader compatibility test unmarshals the migrated provider record.

acceptance_criteria.text: WHEN the built binary runs sworn doctor --fix against a valid unversioned composite containing only a providers map, token, email, tier, and expires_at THE SYSTEM SHALL exit 2, write C-01 with mode 0600, preserve the complete providers map by deep equality, omit the four issuer-less account fields, create no account.json, leave no temporary file, print no field value, and state that fresh sworn login is required (C-01, C-02, N-01, N-08).

WHEN migration is interrupted before temporary write, after temporary write, after sync, after re-read verification, or after atomic rename THE SYSTEM SHALL retain the original composite before rename or the complete C-01 record after rename, and a retry SHALL converge to the identical C-01 bytes with no account.json and no temporary file (C-01, C-02, N-01).

IF the legacy composite is malformed, C-01 or C-02 has an unsupported version, or account.json already exists while legacy account fields remain THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL exit 1, print the secret-safe class and 'run sworn doctor', preserve SHA-256 hashes of credentials.json and account.json, and create no temporary file (C-01, C-02, N-01, N-09).

IF legacy credentials.json contains webhook_url before the C-05 migration or contains any unknown field THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL not replace credentials.json; the coordinator SHALL invoke the C-05 owner for webhook_url and SHALL exit 1 with a secret-safe unclaimed-field class for any unknown field (C-01, C-05, N-01, N-09).

WHEN provider credentials are read or updated after migration THE SYSTEM SHALL access only credentials.json, preserve account.json byte-for-byte, and observe external-process writes rather than a stale process cache (C-01, C-02, N-01).
WHEN an old provider-credentials reader opens the migrated credentials.json THE SYSTEM SHALL still recover the complete providers map while finding no account session fields (C-01, N-01, N-09).

WHILE account.json is corrupt, conflicting, or unsupported THE SYSTEM SHALL leave canonical environment and C-01 provider resolution usable without invoking migration from a provider-only command (C-01, C-02, N-01, N-08).
